explanation: This property ensures that whenever the self-test has been successfully completed and the configurations have been properly loaded (meaning the combined condition of 'selfTestPass' and 'initDone' becomes true), the system will then eventually begin monitoring and reporting health parameters. It also covers the case where this condition is met at the very initial state.

The property states that globally, if both the self-test has been completed successfully and configurations have been loaded properly, then the system must eventually start monitoring and reporting health parameters.

The property states that globally, whenever the self-test has been successfully completed and configurations have been properly loaded (represented by bool_exp1), then in the very next state, the system shall initiate monitoring and reporting of health parameters (represented by bool_exp3). This captures the 'Once...then shall start' behavior, implying an immediate effect in the subsequent time step after the conditions are met.

The property states that globally, whenever the self-test has been successfully completed and the configurations have been loaded properly (bool_exp1), it must eventually be the case that the system starts monitoring and reporting health parameters (bool_exp3). This captures the 'once A and B, then eventually C' requirement.

The property states that globally, whenever the 'selfTestPass' (self-test completed successfully) and 'initDone' (configurations loaded properly) conditions are both true, it is eventually the case that 'startMonitoring' (system starts monitoring health parameters) and 'startReportingHealthParams' (system starts reporting health parameters) are both true. This directly translates the natural language requirement that after the self-test and configuration loading, the system must begin monitoring and reporting.

The property states that globally, whenever the self-test has been completed successfully AND the configurations have been loaded properly, it must eventually be the case that the system starts monitoring health parameters AND starts reporting health parameters. This captures the requirement for the system to initiate monitoring and reporting after successful self-test and configuration loading.

The property states that once the self-test has passed successfully and configurations have been loaded (i.e., when 'selfTestPass' and 'initDone' become true together), then the system shall start monitoring and reporting health parameters in the very next state. The first part of the conjunction, 'G (((! (bool_exp1 & bool_exp2)) & (X (bool_exp1 & bool_exp2))) -> (X bool_exp3))', captures the 'rising edge' of the combined conditions 'selfTestPass' and 'initDone' triggering 'startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams' in the next state. The second part, '((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3)', ensures that if the combined conditions are already true at the initial moment, the monitoring and reporting also start immediately.

The property states that globally, whenever the self-test has passed successfully and the system configurations have been loaded properly, the system must immediately start monitoring and reporting health parameters. This captures the immediate cause-and-effect relationship implied by 'Once X and Y, the system shall start Z'.

This property ensures that once both the self-test has passed (selfTestPass) and the configurations have been loaded (initDone), the system shall immediately start monitoring and reporting health parameters. The LTL formula specifies that globally, whenever the combined condition of self-test passing and configuration loading becomes true (i.e., it transitions from false to true), the system must start monitoring and reporting health parameters in the very next state. Additionally, if the combined condition is already true at the initial state, the monitoring and reporting must also be true at that state.

The property states that globally, whenever the self-test has been completed successfully AND the configurations have been loaded properly, the system must simultaneously start monitoring AND reporting health parameters. This captures the requirement that once the two preconditions are met, the specified actions for monitoring and reporting become true.
